text: {"claims":[{"statement":"The EU permits the use of \"enriched\" furnished cages that must meet certain space and amenity requirements.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The EU permits the use of \"enriched\" [furnished cages](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Furnished_cages \"Furnished cages\") that must meet certain space and amenity requirements.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that the EU allows enriched furnished cages and specifies that they must satisfy space and amenity criteria.","population":"","intervention":"use of enriched furnished cages","comparator":"","outcome":"cages meet certain space and amenity requirements","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Egg producers in many member states have objected to the new quality standards.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Egg producers in many member states have objected to the new quality standards while in some countries, even furnished cages and family cages are subject to be banned as well.","entailment":"The quote reports that egg producers in numerous EU member states have expressed objection to the new quality standards.","population":"egg producers in many member states","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"objected to the new quality standards","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"In all methods of egg production, unwanted male chicks are killed at birth during the process of securing a further generation of egg‑laying hens.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In all methods of egg production, unwanted male chicks are killed at birth during the process of securing a further generation of egg‑laying hens.","entailment":"The quoted sentence asserts that across every egg‑production method, male chicks are killed at birth as part of maintaining the hen population.","population":"all methods of egg production","intervention":"killing of unwanted male chicks at birth","comparator":"","outcome":"removal of male chicks","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"As of June 2023, the practice of killing male chicks has been banned in Germany, France, Luxembourg, and Italy.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"As of June 2023, this practice has been banned in Germany, France, Luxembourg, and Italy.","entailment":"The quote specifies the date (June 2023) and the countries where the practice is prohibited, supporting the claim.","population":"Germany, France, Luxembourg, and Italy","intervention":"ban on killing male chicks","comparator":"","outcome":"practice is prohibited","timeframe":"June 2023","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Some egg producers have begun using in‑ovo sexing to analyze the sex of a chick before it hatches, allowing them to remove male eggs from incubation and avoid chick culling.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Some egg producers have begun using [in-ovo sexing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/In-ovo_sexing \"In-ovo sexing\") to analyze the sex of a chick before it hatches, allowing them to remove male eggs from incubation and avoid chick culling.","entailment":"The sentence reports that certain producers adopt in‑ovo sexing, which lets them identify and discard male eggs prior to hatching, thereby preventing culling.","population":"some egg producers","intervention":"use of in‑ovo sexing to analyze chick sex before hatching","comparator":"","outcome":"removal of male eggs from incubation and avoidance of chick culling","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"As of April 2025, five companies offer commercially available in‑ovo sexing technology, which is used for 28 percent of the European layer population.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"As of April 2025, five companies offer commercially available in-ovo sexing technology, which is used for 28 percent of the European layer population.","entailment":"The quote provides a date, the number of companies offering the technology, and the proportion (28 %) of the European layer population that utilizes it, supporting the claim.","population":"European layer population","intervention":"commercially available in‑ovo sexing technology","comparator":"","outcome":"used for 28 percent of the population","timeframe":"April 2025","quantifiers":"five companies; 28 percent","modality":"descriptive"}]}
